simileAsIfAsThough.note: Three 'as if' uses remain; one 'as though' image was kept as a deliberate concrete flourish.

simileLike.note: Script count includes plain-comparison uses like 'not like this'; figurative load is lower than the raw token count.

changes.before: They rode through fields already cut for harvest, past sheds with doors flung wide for air...
changes.after: Fields already cut for harvest. Sheds open to the air.
Removed; the mare-and-scent sequence already lands the point.

changes.before: Sylvanas did not look where she pointed at once. Her gaze took its sweep first...
changes.after: Sylvanas swept lanes, roofs, alley mouths, the well...

changes.before: sat her horse as if argument were a lesser species
perhaps because the robe and staff told him which one might sound more like the kind of answer he trusted
changes.after: He looked to Jaina. Staff, robe, mage: the kind of proof he trusted.

changes.before: anything unusual was better than one more argument with the village
changes.after: anything strange was better than another argument

changes.before: The gut-first pattern. The households spared the worst because they had eaten differently...
changes.after: Gut first. The spared households eating differently. The sweetness on infected breath.

changes.before: The heavy bread-eaters are failing faster. That's why the lighter cases are the ones living off their own stores.
changes.after: This bread. This flour. The body takes it in a little at a time and it settles where food settles first.

changes.before: Magic had given her the carrier. Sylvanas had given her the path. Together they were enough to move on.
changes.after: Magic had given her the carrier. Sylvanas had given her the path.

changes.before: Not because the answer had grown kinder. Because it had stopped splitting in two.
changes.after: Removed; the square beat already earns the convergence.

changes.before: Can you ask a useful question without sounding wounded by success?
changes.after: Can you ask a useful question without sounding pleased with yourself?

changes.before: Concentrated this time. Narrow. Made, not accidental.
He looked at her as though she had asked who had taught him fear.
changes.after: He looked at her with naked fear.

changes.before: The room was little more than a lockable shed behind the hall, fitted with a bench, a ring in the wall, and a smell of old straw.
changes.after: The room was little more than a lockable shed behind the hall: bench, wall ring, smell of old straw.

changes.before: The man flinched because they were true.
changes.after: Removed; the flinch stands on its own.

changes.before: That's why repeated meals matter. The grain carries the first cut, but people finish the work by living on it.
changes.after: The body helps it. The grain carries the first cut.

flags: Line 79: 'as if she had brought either salvation or insult' kept — first sickroom glance, POV uncertainty earned.
Line 199: 'as if it might answer him' kept — concrete loaf-focus beat, not decorative fog.
Line 461: 'as though grief might bleach it clean' kept — flour-specific image tied to action in scene.

wordcountReduction: Opening pastoral setup cut from a long travel paragraph plus echo beat into four harder beats.
Removed standalone 'Jaina caught it too' because the mare-and-scent sequence already carries the realization.
Compressed Sylvanas's first tactical scan into one clean sweep line.
Shortened the captain's first impression of the newcomers without losing hierarchy or pressure.
Trimmed the medic's appraisal of Jaina from a full sketch to the useful traits only.
Condensed the bread deduction from two explanatory passes to one click and one blunt statement.
Reduced the square convergence beat by cutting thematic summary before the granary move.
Collapsed the lockup-room setup into bench/ring/straw essentials.
Cut the exit recap so the chapter leaves on forward pressure toward Andorhal instead of re-explaining the mechanism.
hitlistPatterns: Removed hedge stack around the captain turning to Jaina: no more 'perhaps / might / kind of' pileup.
Replaced comparison fog in the captain read: Sylvanas now sits her horse above argument instead of inside a writerly simile.
Killed statement-then-restatement in the bread diagnosis dialogue.
Cut negation scaffolding in the partnership beat after the public reveal.
Replaced Tomas's fear simile with a direct physical read.
Cut causal gloss after Sylvanas's warning to the captain; the man's reaction now does its own work.
Reduced contact-verb clustering by trimming extra catch/press beats during the reveal and interrogation sections.
Lowered filter-word load from 6 to 3 by removing explicit seeing/noticing where POV already implied them.
Lowered breath-tell load from 5 to 3 by stripping one exhale beat and other respiratory filler.
Checked remaining 'like' and 'as if/as though' uses individually; only the concrete ones stayed.
dialogueRegister: Checked every quoted line in the chapter for document voice and lab-report phrasing; none survived.
Kept Jaina's diagnosis plain: grain, bread, body, road, loads.
Kept Sylvanas in command shorthand and deadpan pressure rather than formal tactical exposition.
Replaced the one modern-sounding snark line ('wounded by success') with a sharper in-world bite.
Preserved the captain's village register and the medic's blunt practicality; neither sounds like a memo.
Preserved one-word and two-word pressure hits ('Useful. Stay here.' / 'Bread.' / 'No.') because they match voice-sheet cadence.
